description_long: This street food stall is a compact, wheeled cart with a traditional Japanese design, featuring a wooden frame painted in a muted pink color. The stall has a black canopy roof and is adorned with two cylindrical paper lanterns on either side, adding to its authentic street food ambiance. The front of the stall displays a large signboard with Japanese characters and illustrations, while the side counter extends outward, providing space for serving or preparing food. The cart is supported by two large spoked wheels and a metal handlebar for mobility, and the main counter area is equipped with small bowls and cups, suggesting food service. The surface of the stall is mostly flat and matte, with visible wood grain texture, and the overall structure is sturdy yet portable. Decorative elements and menu signage enhance its visual appeal, making it suitable for urban street settings.
description: A traditional Japanese-style street food cart with a pink wooden frame, black canopy, paper lanterns, and prominent signage, featuring a side serving counter and large wheels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main signboard with Japanese text, two lanterns, the serving counter with bowls, and the large wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended serving counter, a single lantern, and the handlebar for pushing the cart.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel, both wheels, and the open serving area with lanterns visible on each side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the menu signage, a lantern, and the side counter with a bowl and cup.
attribute_signage: Large pink signboard with white Japanese characters and illustrations, mounted above the main counter; additional menu signage with red and white graphics on the side counter.
attribute_serving_counter: A rectangular wooden counter extends from the side of the cart, providing a flat surface for serving; it is equipped with small bowls and cups and has a matte finish.
attribute_food_display: Food items are presented openly on the counter in bowls and cups, with no visible protective covering or display case.
